name: lsp-agent-tools
description: Exposes language server capabilities as Language Model Tools for AI agents — hover, document symbols, call hierarchy, code actions, workspace symbols, signature help, and type definitions.

languageModelTools.name: lsp_hover
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get type information, documentation, and signatures for a symbol at a specific location in a file. Uses the language server's hover provider. Returns type signatures, doc comments, parameter descriptions, inferred types. Use this instead of reading entire files when you need to know what type a variable is or what a function returns.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get type info and documentation for a symbol via the language server

filePath.description: Absolute path or workspace-relative path to the file containing the symbol

symbol.description: Name of the symbol to hover over (e.g. 'loadConfig', 'port', 'arr.map')

lineContent.description: A substring of the line where the symbol appears. Used to disambiguate when the symbol appears multiple times.

line.description: 1-based line number where the symbol appears. Optional — used when symbol appears multiple times.

languageModelTools.name: lsp_documentSymbols
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get a structured outline of all symbols in a file — functions, classes, structs, enums, methods, variables, constants. Returns a hierarchical tree showing nesting (methods inside classes, etc.) with line ranges. Use this instead of reading an entire file when you need to understand its structure. Optionally filter by symbol kind.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get a structured symbol outline of a file via the language server

filePath.description: Absolute path or workspace-relative path to the file

kind.description: Filter by symbol kind. Options: all, function, class, method, variable, constant, struct, enum, interface, namespace, property. Default: all.

languageModelTools.name: lsp_typeDefinition
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Navigate from a variable or expression to the definition of its type. Given 'let target: DeployTarget = ...', this finds where DeployTarget is defined (not where target is declared). Use this when you need to understand the shape of a type — its fields, variants, or interface.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Navigate to the definition of a symbol's type via the language server

filePath.description: Absolute path or workspace-relative path to the file

symbol.description: Name of the variable/expression whose type you want to find

lineContent.description: A substring of the line where the symbol appears

languageModelTools.name: lsp_callHierarchy
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the incoming callers and/or outgoing callees of a function. Use 'incoming' to find who calls this function (impact analysis before changing a signature). Use 'outgoing' to find what this function calls. Supports depth up to 3 levels. This provides directed call relationships, not just references.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get incoming callers and outgoing callees for a function via the language server

filePath.description: Absolute path or workspace-relative path to the file

lineContent.description: A substring of the line where the symbol is defined or called

direction.description: Which direction to traverse: 'incoming' (who calls this), 'outgoing' (what this calls), or 'both'. Default: 'both'.

depth.description: How many levels deep to traverse (1-3). Default: 1.

languageModelTools.name: lsp_workspaceSymbols
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search for symbols by name across the entire workspace using the language server's symbol index. Finds functions, classes, structs, enums, interfaces, variables, and constants. Supports partial matching. Returns symbol kind, location, and container. Use this for semantic symbol search that understands re-exports and aliases.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search for symbols by name across the workspace via the language server

query.description: Symbol name to search for. Supports partial matching (e.g. 'Config' matches 'loadConfig', 'ConfigManager')

kind.description: Filter by symbol kind. Options: all, function, class, method, variable, constant, struct, enum, interface, namespace, property. Default: all.

languageModelTools.name: lsp_codeActions
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Query available code actions (quick fixes, refactorings) at a specific line in a file. When a diagnostic exists, the language server often has high-quality fixes already computed (add import, fix typo, etc.). Optionally apply a specific action by title. Use this before inventing your own fix for a diagnostic.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Query and apply quick fixes and refactorings via the language server

filePath.description: Absolute path or workspace-relative path to the file

line.description: 1-based line number to query code actions at

diagnosticMessage.description: Filter to code actions for a specific diagnostic (substring match)

kind.description: Filter by action kind: quickfix, refactor, source, or all. Default: all.

apply.description: Title of a specific code action to apply. If set, the action is executed instead of just listed.

languageModelTools.name: lsp_signatureHelp
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get function parameter information — names, types, order, and documentation. This provides call-site context including which parameter is active and all overloads. Use this when you need to know how to call a function correctly.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get function parameter info via the language server

filePath.description: Absolute path or workspace-relative path to the file

symbol.description: Name of the function being called

lineContent.description: A substring of the line where the function call appears
